title: Stagnant Reservoir
master_statement: A stagnant reservoir is stably stratified with two immiscible liquids of density \(\rho_{1}\)and \(\rho_{2}\) where \(\rho_{1} > \rho_{2}\), respectively. Which graph below depicts correctly the distribution of gauge pressure along a vertical line through the liquids.

worked_solutions.text: As \(\rho_{1} > \rho_{2}\), the pressure in layer 1 will increase faster with increasing depth than in layer 2, ie the slope is steeper.
At the interface between the two liquids the pressure has to be identical; otherwise the fluid would accelerate, which is in violation of the assumption that the fluid is static.
In consideration of the arguments above, the correct pressure diagram is displaced in diagram (ii)
